name: The Pedagogical Engine
description: The Atlas framework for generating interactive learning paths and exploring the historical influence graph of programming.

name: Programming Language Generations
description: A classification of programming languages based on their historical era, hardware constraints, and industry needs.

name: Guided Odyssey
description: Curated narrative journeys through the Language Atlas that guide students from foundational precursors to modern systems.

name: Influence Graph
description: The directed acyclic graph (DAG) representing how programming languages have influenced one another across history.

name: Semantic Search
description: Full-text search capabilities across the Atlas using FTS5 to find deep connections between languages, people, and concepts.

name: Zenith State
description: The mature phase of the Language Atlas where it serves as a high-density, authoritative research platform.

name: The Software Crisis
description: The recurring historical realization that software complexity has outpaced hardware capabilities and programmer productivity, driving the invention of new paradigms.
